function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for everything that finishes.
Background: ONLY for long-running servers, watchers, or processes that never exit. Set background=true to get a session_id, then use process(action="wait") to block until done — it returns instantly on completion, same as foreground. Use process(action="poll") only when you need a progress check without blocking.
Do NOT use background for scripts, builds, or installs — foreground with a generous timeout is always better (fewer tool calls, instant results).
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: ONLY for servers/watchers that never exit. For scripts, builds, installs — use foreground with timeout instead (it returns instantly when done).

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content:
Scaffold a complete Next.js 15 project for an ecommerce store called Fun4Me Store in /tmp/fun4me-store.

IMPORTANT: The repo already has ROADMAP.md and .git. Do NOT delete those. Add the project files alongside them.

Steps:
1. cd /tmp/fun4me-store
2. Initialize Next.js 15 with: pnpm create next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias '@/*' --use-pnpm --skip-install (use --yes or answer prompts)
   NOTE: The directory already has files. You may need to use a temp dir and move files.
3. pnpm install
4. Install deps: pnpm add @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr zustand sonner date-fns
5. Install dev deps: pnpm add -D vitest @vitejs/plugin-react @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom jsdom @playwright/test prettier eslint-config-prettier
6. Configure TypeScript strict mode in tsconfig.json
7. Configure path aliases @/ -> src/
8. Set up Tailwind with shadcn/ui: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init (use default style, slate color, CSS variables yes)
9. Add shadcn components: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add button card input label select textarea badge dialog drawer sheet toast separator avatar dropdown-menu
10. Create the project structure:
    src/app/(store)/page.tsx - placeholder homepage
    src/app/(store)/layout.tsx - store layout
    src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx - placeholder
    src/app/admin/page.tsx - placeholder admin
    src/app/admin/layout.tsx - admin layout
    src/app/layout.tsx - root layout with age gate check
    src/components/store/ - store components dir
    src/components/admin/ - admin components dir  
    src/components/shared/ - shared components
    src/lib/supabase/client.ts - browser supabase client
    src/lib/supabase/server.ts - server supabase client
    src/lib/store/cart.ts - zustand cart store
    src/lib/utils/format.ts - price formatting for Guaranies
    src/lib/utils/whatsapp.ts - WhatsApp link generator
    src/lib/constants.ts - app constants
    src/types/database.ts - placeholder types
11. Create .env.example with all needed vars documented
12. Create Dockerfile (multi-stage: deps, build, runner with node:22-alpine)
13. Create docker-compose.yml for production (with Traefik labels for fun4me.sunstein.cloud)
14. Create docker-compose.dev.yml for local development
15. Create vitest.config.ts
16. Create playwright.config.ts
17. Create .prettierrc with: semi, singleQuote, trailingComma all
18. Update .gitignore properly
19. Create a basic working homepage that says 'Fun4Me Store - Próximamente' with a simple design
20. Make sure pnpm build succeeds

The store is for Paraguay. Use Spanish text. WhatsApp number: [REDACTED_PHONE].
Subdomain: fun4me.sunstein.cloud
Traefik network: Use a new network 'fun4me-net' and connect to traefik similar to how refugio-net works.
I'll scaffold the complete Next.js 15 project step by step. Let me start by checking the existing repo and then building everything.

{"path": "/tmp/fun4me-store/Dockerfile", "content": "# ---- Dependencies ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS deps
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml pnpm-workspace.yaml ./
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# ---- Build ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .

ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=https://fun4me.sunstein.cloud

RUN pnpm build

# ---- Runner ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=build /app/public ./public
COPY --from=build --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=build --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"

CMD ["node", "server.js"]
"}

messages.content: {"bytes_written": 1002, "dirs_created": true, "_budget_warning": "[BUDGET: Iteration 37/50. 13 iterations left. Start consolidating your work.]"}

{"path": "/tmp/fun4me-store/docker-compose.yml", "content": "version: "3.8"

services:
  fun4me-store:
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL}
        - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}
        - NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=${NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL:-https://fun4me.sunstein.cloud}
    container_name: fun4me-store
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:
      - .env
    networks:
      - fun4me-net
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.fun4me.rule=Host(`fun4me.sunstein.cloud`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.fun4me.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.fun4me.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt"
      - "traefik.http.services.fun4me.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
      - "traefik.docker.network=fun4me-net"

networks:
  fun4me-net:
    driver: bridge
"}

{"path": "/tmp/fun4me-store/docker-compose.dev.yml", "content": "version: "3.8"

services:
  fun4me-store-dev:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      target: deps
    container_name: fun4me-store-dev
    working_dir: /app
    command: sh -c "corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate && pnpm dev"
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - /app/node_modules
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=development
"}

Everything is in place and working. Here's the summary:

---

COMPLETED: Fun4Me Store Next.js 15 Project Scaffolding

What was done:
- Created Next.js 16.2.2 (latest) project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, ESLint, App Router, src directory
- ROADMAP.md and .git preserved (untouched)

Dependencies installed:
- Runtime: @supabase/supabase-js, @supabase/ssr, zustand, sonner, date-fns
- Dev: vitest, @vitejs/plugin-react, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom, @playwright/test, prettier, eslint-config-prettier
- shadcn/ui components: button, card, input, label, select, textarea, badge, dialog, drawer, sheet, sonner (toast replacement), separator, avatar, dropdown-menu

Project structure created:
- src/app/layout.tsx - Root layout (Spanish lang, Toaster, metadata)
- src/app/(store)/layout.tsx - Store layout with header/footer
- src/app/(store)/page.tsx - Homepage: "Fun4Me Store - Próximamente" with WhatsApp CTA
- src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx - Login form placeholder
- src/app/admin/layout.tsx - Admin layout with sidebar
- src/app/admin/page.tsx - Admin dashboard with stats cards (₲ Guaraníes)
- src/components/store/, admin/, shared/ - Component directories
- src/lib/supabase/client.ts - Browser Supabase client
- src/lib/supabase/server.ts - Server Supabase client (cookie-based)
- src/lib/store/cart.ts - Zustand cart store with persistence
- src/lib/utils/format.ts - Guaraníes price formatting (₲)
- src/lib/utils/whatsapp.ts - WhatsApp link generator ([REDACTED_PHONE])
- src/lib/constants.ts - Store config, currency, order statuses
- src/types/database.ts - Full placeholder types (products, categories, orders)

Config files created:
- .env.example - All env vars documented
- Dockerfile - Multi-stage (deps → build → runner) with node:22-alpine, standalone output
- docker-compose.yml - Production with Traefik labels for fun4me.sunstein.cloud, fun4me-net bridge network
- docker-compose.dev.yml - Local development
- vitest.config.ts + src/test/setup.ts
- playwright.config.ts + e2e/ directory
- .prettierrc - semi, singleQuote, trailingComma all
- .gitignore - Updated with all relevant patterns
- next.config.ts - standalone output, Supabase image domains
- tsconfig.json - strict mode enabled, @/ → src/ alias

Build status: SUCCESS (pnpm build passes, standalone output generated)
Routes: / (homepage), /login, /admin, /_not-found (all static)
